entry.title: Re: Wrong format in excel download
entry.content:
Hello,

OLE technology is not available within browsers, consequently not available within webgui/Fiori

  • OLE Automation Controller

The controlling of an OLE automation server is not possible.

  • Office Integration

Because Office Integration is based on OLE Automation (a legacy Microsoft Windows technology not available within browsers) it is limited within SAP GUI for HTML. Office Documents can only be viewed but not be changed. 

 

The only workaround available:

3314872 - SAP Desktop Office Integration Support for SAP GUI for HTML and SAP GUI for Java

-> https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-sap/how-to-enable-the-sap-desktop-office-integration-for-sap-gui-for-html-and/ba-p/13566119

entry.title: Where can I start learning principles and strategies of SAP Ariba Category Management?
entry.content:
I recommend to access our Introducing SAP Ariba Category Management Fundamentals learning journey.

Overview
This course will teach the learner how to create, manage, and monitor category-specific strategies to achieve their organization’s procurement goals.

Learning objectives
Upon the completion of this learning journey, participants will gain insight into how a Category Manager creates a category profile by classifying a product category, uploading company policies, laws, and regulations concerning that specific category, and adding team members and stakeholders that will assist with the execution of the strategy. Then, develop a strategy and plan, including category and market analysis, strategy and implementation, and approval. Finally, monitor the strategy execution to see if goals are met, initiatives are on target, and upcoming events are posted in the planning calendar. 

Prerequisites

  • Learning Journey: Discovering Category Management

Pass all the quizzes and receive a digital badge

Please ask a question related to the digital learning Journey in the Q&A area. 

Our SAP Learning Experts will get back to you as soon as possible! We are here to support you. 

I appreciate your feedback and we will make sure to continue sharing interesting topics.

Kind regards
Margit

entry.title: Re: 404 error while employee applying for a job from inte...
entry.content:
Difficult to help without investigation of the problem.

Anyway, suggestion from my side is to try this:

  • disable ICS (by CSB) in both CSB and RCM
  • post an internal job on ICS (RCM)
  • access an Internal candidate profile in ICS (RCM)
  • perform a job search on ICS (RCM) career site
  • if above works, enable CSB (by CSB) back

 

Regards,

Piotr

entry.title: Re: Custom CDS Views - Using Multiple Data Sources
entry.content:
Hello @Gerd_x Gerd,

I just tested this in a cloud system and do not get the error that you mentioned. You may want to open a ticket with PM-WOC-MN to get support from the application colleagues that provide the view.

Generally, I would not recommend adding fields by following such deep paths. Apart from this, you will multiply your result set as you will get the text in ALL languages unless you filter by the language afterwards. Try adding the text view by defining your own 1..1 association directly to it instead. This may also resolve this inconsistency error.

Best Regards
Andreas

entry.title: Extending the reach of enterprise systems in communication challenged environments
entry.content:
For over two decades SAP has been providing solutions to customers in defence and other industries the ability to operate in communication challenged environments. SAP has worked directly with customers as well as through the Defence Interest Group which represents our military customers and provide consolidated requirements on this topic. While advances have been made in establishing pervasive networks, be it garrison or at remote sites, there are still several situations where connectivity to enterprise systems prevents direct access to them:

  • the type of connectivity often varies by location or situation
  • data transmission costs may be too high for limited budgets
  • data transmission is degraded or not permitted for safety or operational reasons
  • transmission protocols restrict how data can be transmitted and stored
  • backend systems are not available (planned or unplanned)

These situations can occur within the national boundaries of a customer or when they operate elsewhere around the globe.

In contrast to individuals, teams have the added requirement to share data and information even in communication challenged environments. This is because enterprise processes are typically conducted by more than one individual and as such, there is a need for shared situational awareness. For example, when fulfilling demand, one must wait till the goods receipt has been recorded to record a goods issue, especially when it’s important to track against batch or serial numbers.

As such, people need to continue to work in these communications challenged environments:

  • individuals for a few minutes or hours while they are away from their “desk” working in zones without network connectivity. These could be on base or at remote site on operation or exercise where there is little to no network or it’s unsafe to transmit due to the proximity to explosives. Another example is an air force crew bringing an offline device to keep track of maintenance activities and parts consumed while away on missions. Typically, these individuals would synchronize after their return to their “desk”. SAP customers, across a multitude of industries, rely on SAP applications to enable their remote field workers to maintain their own and their customer assets.
  • Teams for a few days to one or several weeks away from their home base. This could be a team deployed on exercise or operation such as in humanitarian assistance, peacekeeping, or higher intensity operation. Be it a land, air, naval or joint deployment. Small teams such as on a small vessel or an air task group or much larger deployments. These include naval customers who manage the logistics process on board their vessels on deployments lasting multiple months. Similarly, teams in the commercial shipping and oil & gas industries use SAP solution to manage their logistics processes offline.

 

Typically, synchronization occurs on a periodic basis (e.g. daily or less frequently) or on an ad-hoc basis whenever individuals or teams can establish a connection to synchronize. In rare cases, they would synchronize only upon their return to their home base. There are no technical limits to how long individuals and teams can operate without synchronizations. This is because at some point there is a need for these individuals or teams to request supplies or maintenance as they are no longer fully self-sufficient.

Broadly, customers look to SAP for the following to enable logistics processes:

  • Provide individuals and teams the ability to have access to business essential functions (i.e. a subset) of their enterprise system while operating in communication challenged environments
  • Provide end-users a resilient, modern, and consistent user experience across devices
  • Provide a resilient, modern, and consistent technology platform that customers can extend and integrate with other systems (e.g. IoT and Battle Management) as well as manage and support them in a distributed IT landscape

SAP S/4HANA Defense & Security and SAP Service & Asset Manager are the key enablers for teams and individuals respectively.

These products provide technology to establish and synchronize data across the communication challenged landscape:

  • Identify the sufficient master and transactional data subset from the enterprise system to be transferred to the system of the individual and the team to minimize the IT footprint
  • Detect and synchronize new and changed data to the relevant recipients while maintaining transactional integrity
  • Establish mechanisms to minimize the occurrences of data synchronization errors from data conflicts

Our customers can operate in a variety of communication challenged environments and provide the tools for users to seamlessly work regardless of the status of the network. The IT organization has in their hands familiar technologies to manage their SAP landscape. Finally, Commanders at all levels can make better readiness based decisions from data from the edge.

entry.title: Experience the Benefits of SAP Software with our new Interactive Value Journeys
entry.content:
Introduction

 

The International Business Unit Defense and Security (D&S) is delighted to announce that the new Interactive Value Journeys (IVJs) for the Defense and Security industry are now publicly accessible online. These IVJs provide comprehensive insights and tailored solutions, empowering organizations within the industry to leverage best practices and innovative strategies for overcoming their organizational challenges and problems. By making these resources available, SAP aims to support the continuous improvement and operational excellence of our clients, thereby contributing to the overall security and stability of global Defense and Security industry.

 

 

What are Interactive Value Journeys and for what can they be used?

 

Figure 1: Interacive Value Journey for Operational Readiness

 

Interactive Value Journeys (IVJs) are advanced, interactive customer journeys or demos that allow us to showcase the following to customers, prospective clients, partners, and other interested parties:

  • Core Products and Solutions: An overview of the core products and solutions SAP currently offers for Defense and Security customers, including armed forces organizations, emergency response teams, border control, and customs services.

  • Key Advantages and Differentiation: Highlighting the main advantages of SAP’s offerings and how they differ from competitors' solutions.

  • Realistic Implementation Scenarios: Demonstrating typical implementation scenarios, providing a realistic view of how these solutions can be deployed and utilized.

  • Recent Innovations: Showcasing SAP's latest innovations and advancements tailored for the D&S industry.

 

 

The new Defense and Security Interactive Value Journeys

 

Figure 2: Example Interactive Value Journey

 

In total, we offer 9 distinct IVJs covering the following topic areas:

1. More Effective Force Generation and Deployment with SAP: Link

 

Figure 3: More Effective Force Generation and Deployment with SAP IVJ

 

This IVJ demonstrates how SAP S/4HANA Defense and Security enables our clients to digitize and enhance the force generation and force deployment process holistically with our ERP software solution. By implementing our Enterprise Resource Planning software, our clients can benefit from structured real-time management of their forces, vehicles (reaching from cars and trucks, over ships to airplanes), and other assets during day-to-day operations, trainings, exercises, or active missions. SAP S/4HANA also ensures maximum interoperability within and beyond the organization, facilitating seamless coordination with alliances and partners.

 

2. Ensure Full Operational Readiness of your D&S Organization: Link 

 

Figure 4: Ensure Full Operational Readiness of your D&S Organization IVJ

 

Operational readiness is highly critical for any Defense and Security (D&S) organization, whether military or emergency services. With its decades-long experience in this and other industries, SAP enables the monitoring, administration, and optimization of operational readiness for each unit, as well as the readiness of vehicles, equipment, and other assets, using a centralized, intuitive software application. This IVJ showcases a typical scenario in this area, highlighting the benefits of such solutions and demonstrating how they can be effectively implemented by clients.

 

3. Strengthen and Secure your Critical Supply Chains with SAP: Link

 

Figure 5: Strengthen and Secure your Critical Supply Chains with SAP IVJ

 

SAP is a leading software vendor in the areas of Procurement, Transportation, Warehousing, and Supply Chain Management. It offers Defense and Security organizations access to best-in-class software solutions, thereby enabling for an end-to-end optimization of their logistics processes. Our clients benefit from a wide range of innovations, such as Artificial Intelligence functions and features, automated document processing, and the direct integration of Internet of Things (IoT), Digital Twin technology, or Robotic Process Automation (RPA) devices into the system. This IVJ demonstrates a typical scenario of implementing a logistics software solution in a D&S organization.

 

4. Create Seamless Interoperability Across Organizations: Link 

 

Figure 6: Create Seamless Interoperability Across Organizations IVJ

 

SAP Enterprise Resource Planning (ERP) solutions for Defense and Security (D&S) organizations are built on the foundation of maximum interoperability. From an IT perspective, this includes the ability to deploy the software as a cloud or on-premises solution and to develop customized applications for specific processes and tasks. Moreover, it enables the direct integration of all core stakeholders into the system by using existing functions and interfacing, or by integrating specific APIs. This integration extends beyond IT functionality to encompass operational partners such as suppliers in the procurement area, as well as alliances and partners. The interoperable architecture of SAP S/4HANA Defense and Security facilitates real-time data exchange between two or more D&S organizations, allowing them to jointly conduct operations, trainings, exercises, and active missions. This functionality is available even in communication-challenged environments through the SAP Disconnected Operations solutions. Our IVJ for interoperability highlights the various advantages of such solutions for D&S clients.

 

5. SAP Business AI for Defense and Security Organizations: Link 

 

Figure 7: SAP Business AI for Defense and Security Organizations IVJ

 

Artificial Intelligence has been a global megatrend since the emergence of OpenAI's Generative AI-powered ChatGPT application in late 2022. What many people might not know is that SAP has been a leading player in the field of Artificial Intelligence, offering numerous Machine Learning and traditional AI solutions to its clients for a long time. To further enhance its applications and services for clients, SAP has decided to integrate Artificial Intelligence, and here in particular Generative Artificial Intelligence, into all of its core solutions and products. Examples of this include Embedded AI functions within applications like S/4HANA, the SAP Joule Copilot, which allows users to interact with applications in natural language, as well as many more innovations. The above Interactive Value Journey for SAP Business AI highlights the AI products and services offered to Defense and Security clients, potential implementation scenarios, and the advantages our customers can gain with the technology.

 

6. SAP Sustainability Solutions for D&S Organizations: Link

 

Figure 8: SAP Sustainability Solutions for D&S Organizations IVJ

 

Sustainability is a top priority for the majority of governments, companies, and organizations globally. SAP is proud that its SAP Sustainability Solutions are positioned in the Leaders category for worldwide carbon accounting and management software applications in the 2024 IDC MarketScape Vendor Assessment. The above IVJ provides insights into how D&S organizations can use our sustainability software to monitor, analyze, and manage all aspects of sustainability in the most efficient and transparent way possible. This enables our customers to meet international, national, and internal environmental, sustainability, and climate goals, thereby enhancing operational readiness, mission effectiveness, and contributing to global security and stability.

 

7. SAP Employee Health & Wellbeing Solutions: Link 

 

Figure 9: SAP Employee Health and Wellbeing Solutions IVJ

 

With this Interactive Value Journey, we aim to inform interested parties about the possibilities and benefits of SAP Employee Health & Wellbeing solutions. This comprehensive software package enables organizations to enhance the wellbeing, safety, and health of their entire workforce, regardless of their size. The IVJ illustrates how D&S organizations can use these solutions to address challenges such as high rates of sick leave, employee turnover, and general workplace safety issues, while simultaneously making the employer more attractive to potential applicants. SAP Employee Health & Wellbeing solutions streamline the management of organizational wellbeing by integrating distributed systems and data into a unified application. This integration facilitates the monitoring and improvement of each employee's wellbeing within the organization. Additionally, it helps identify critical pain points, such as employee safety concerns, ultimately enhancing employee retention by creating better working conditions.

8. Improve your Auditing and Reporting Processes with SAP: Link 

 

Figure 10: Improve your Auditing and Reporting Processes with SAP

 

The above IVJ showcases how the SAP Auditing and Reporting solutions, as an important part of the SAP Financial Management portfolio, can empower Defense and Security (D&S) organizations to achieve greater transparency and operational efficiency. These solutions enable D&S organizations to adhere to various external and internal compliance standards while enhancing their risk management from day-to-day operations to specific international missions. For example, they facilitate the constant monitoring of critical projects within the organization and ensure adherence to internal rules and guidelines, such as budget restrictions. Overall, this can help D&S organizations reinforce trust and accountability within their operations.

 

9. Detecting and Mitigating Natural Catastrophes with EDiSON: Link

 

Figure 11: Detecting and Mitigating Natural Catastrophes with EDiSON

 

Disaster prevention and effective disaster response, such as responding to natural catastrophes, are typically among the core tasks of Defense and Security organizations. For this reason, SAP has teamed up with Japan's Oita University to offer its clients the innovative, AI-powered Earth Disaster Intelligent System Operational Network (EDiSON). This software application measurably enhances the detection and mitigation of natural disasters. As demonstrated in the IVJ, EDiSON includes various innovative and helpful functions and features, such as a comprehensive dashboard, the ability to create forecasts for the course of extreme weather events, and different forms of impact analyses. The AI-driven EDiSON solution thereby relies on the SAP Business Technology Platform (BTP) for services like automation, analytics, Artificial Intelligence, data management, and application development.

 

 

Further Information

 

If you have any additional questions about SAP Defense and Security software solutions, please visit our website or reach out directly to us by clicking on the following link, or by adding a comment to this blog post.

Additional information about the SAP Business Applications, and the most recent offerings and innovations from SAP can be found below:

  • SAP Enterprise Resource Planning Website 
  • RISE with SAP Website 
  • GROW with SAP Website 
  • SAP Business AI Website 
  • SAP Unstoppable Website 
  • SAP Sustainability Solutions Website 
  • SAP Industry Solutions Website 
  • SAP Partner Portal

entry.title: Design Reports - How many days ago the last sales order was created
entry.content:
Hello Design Reports Experts!!

I would like to create an index (Key Figure) to display how many days ago the last sales order was created for the customer via Key Figure in a report.
The idea would be to first perform a filter to list only the last sales order created for the customer, and then use the “created on” field of the sales order to perform the calculation.

Example image:
Customer placed the last order on 12/3/2024.
Today's date: 12/5/2024
Expected result in a given Key Figure: 2 days
Idea of ​​calculation to be performed: 12/5/2024 - 12/3/2024 = difference of 2 days.

Thanks a lot for the help.

Clóvis Müller

entry.title: The importance of CNAME, Certificates and Custom API Domain Prefix
entry.content:
Hello SAP Community! 

Securing customer data while providing a smooth and reliable user experience is essential for every business. SAP Customer Data Cloud offers solutions that not only protect sensitive information but also ensure brand consistency and user trust through configurations like CNAME, SSL certificates, and custom API domain prefixes.

A CNAME (Canonical Name) acts as an alias, connecting your domain’s subdomain to another domain. Within SAP Customer Data Cloud, this setup eliminates browser warnings about third-party cookies, an issue frequently encountered in privacy-focused browsers like Safari or during incognito browsing. By using a CNAME, API calls and social logins appear to come directly from your domain, keeping interactions branded and professional.

Adding SSL certificates to SAP Customer Data Cloud strengthens data encryption, protecting exchanges between users, systems, and the platform. For example, creating a CNAME like identity.mysite.com ensures that all interactions route securely through your domain, avoiding cookie-blocking issues and enhancing the user experience.

The platform also enables configurations like trusted sites and custom API domain prefixes for added security and control. Trusted sites ensure all domains involved in your data exchanges are verified, while a custom API domain prefix—like identity.mysite.com—ensures your branding is reflected in every interaction. These configurations are ideal for features like social logins, hosted pages, centralized login, and Single Sign-On (SSO).

With SAP Customer Data Cloud, these configurations go beyond enhancing security. They enable seamless integrations with external platforms like Facebook and Apple, comply with evolving browser standards, and improve customer journeys across mobile and desktop devices. Implementing these best practices ensures a secure, branded, and user-friendly experience.

To discover how SAP Customer Data Cloud can transform your user experience and help configure these features, visit The importance of CNAME, Certificates and Custom API Domain Prefix on the SAP Learning Hub.

Access and Support

To access the link above, complete a one-time registration in SAP Learning Hub for SAP Enterprise Support. A step-by-step guide is available here.

Need Help?

Have questions? Reach out to the SAP Value Map team or sign up for the SAP Enterprise Support Value Maps to explore additional resources.

entry.title: Re: CCM - Settlement document with negative and positive...
entry.content: hello yes i did!  there is two alternatives  alternative 1 - i didnt test this one Settlement Management   -> Condition Contract Management ->  Condition Contract Settlement -> Define and Configure Profiles for Business Volume Determination Move the value into an existing header field which you don’t use, e.g. WBRK-KIDNO. Alternatively create a new Z-field. Use the SHKZG to head.  And i did not know this at the time and the alternative that i did was ABAP.  I did it via enhancement WB2_CC_SETTL_CHANGE_FINALLY, I did the code below, if you need it. Code used:     READ TABLE ct_komzrke INTO ls_komzrke INDEX 1.     IF ls_komzrke-lfart = '0S12'.       LOOP AT ct_komzrpe INTO ls_komzrpe.         lv_oldnr = ls_komzrpe-oldnr.         IF ls_komzrpe-itemcat = 1.           ls_komzrpe-oldnr = ls_komzrpe-oldnr + 1.           MODIFY ct_komzrpe FROM ls_komzrpe INDEX sy-tabix.           LOOP AT ct_komlfkoec INTO ls_komlfkoec                                WHERE oldpo = ls_komzrpe-oldpo.             ls_komlfkoec-oldnr = ls_komzrpe-oldnr.             MODIFY ct_komlfkoec FROM ls_komlfkoec INDEX sy-tabix.           ENDLOOP.           READ TABLE ct_komzrke INTO ls_komzrke                                 WITH KEY oldnr = ls_komzrpe-oldnr.           IF sy-subrc <> 0.             READ TABLE ct_komzrke INTO ls_komzrke                         WITH KEY oldnr = lv_oldnr.             ls_komzrke-oldnr = ls_komzrpe-oldnr.             APPEND ls_komzrke TO ct_komzrke.           ENDIF.         ENDIF.       ENDLOOP.     ENDIF.

entry.title: Master Data Management: The Foundation for Modern Enterprise Data Architecture
entry.content:
Within an enterprise, critical data such as customer information, product details, asset records, and location data is used across various business units and operational systems. Managing this "master data" to ensure its accuracy, timeliness, and consistency is the core function of Master Data Management (MDM). This process, complemented by robust data governance practices, ensures that the responsibility for data quality is appropriately managed and verified. Over the past two decades, the software industry has provided systems to support MDM, but what functionalities are essential for a modern MDM system today?

Core Functions of a Modern MDM System

Data Integration and Cleansing

A modern MDM system should access data from operational systems and bring it together in a master data hub. This involves loading and synchronizing data, cleansing it to correct errors, and enriching records—for instance, filling missing postal codes, tax IDs or adding demographic information – leveraging data quality services, like SAP Data Quality Management, microservices for location data, or third party data providers, like CDQ (Corporate Data Quality).

Data Matching and Merging

With master data distributed across numerous systems, identifying duplicate records is crucial. The system should be capable of merging candidate records into a single, trusted master record (sometimes called the “best record” or “golden record”) through predefined rules based on criteria like how recently they were updated or their completeness and, sometimes, human intervention.

Data Governance and Workflow

A modern MDM system should support data governance processes, including access controls, data ownership, and audit trails. Automated, customizable workflow features for data stewards to manage data issues and approvals for changes are also essential. This allows various teams to own unique master data attributes and enforce validated values for specific data points through collaborative workflow routing and notification.

Hierarchy and Classification Management

Managing hierarchies of classification (e.g., legal business hierarchies, retail shop locations, customer classifications) is a key feature. The system should handle complex, unbalanced hierarchies efficiently, including the ability to retrieve legal hierarchies from external data providers like a Dun & Bradstreet so that the MDM system can load that data, solve any conflicts, and replicate the hierarchy information to consuming applications.

Analytics and Reporting

The system should provide intuitive analytics and reporting capabilities, highlighting data quality metrics and trends over time.  The MDM solution should allow data experts to define, validate, and monitor established business rules to confirm master data readiness and quality.  There should also be the ability analyze the performance of master data management processes (i.e. number of open master data change requests or the average time to close a change request).

Scalability and Deployment Options

A modern MDM system should be scalable and support both on-premise and cloud-based deployment options, adapting as source data migrates between different platforms.

Security and Usability

The system should have role-based security, ensuring only authorized users can perform specific tasks. An intuitive, customizable user interface and industry or data domain-specific templates can speed up deployment.

Supporting Current Initiatives

A robust MDM system supports digitalization, cloud migration, and business process automation. It also provides high-quality data for training AI models, making the AI more effective. As businesses increasingly adopt generative AI, having a reliable MDM system ensures that the AI models are trained on accurate, trustworthy data – and the most important data is your business-critical master data.

MDM and Data Fabric

The data fabric approach aims to provide business users direct access to corporate data assets without traditional data warehouses. However, the semantic layer of a data fabric must navigate numerous underlying operational systems to determine the most up-to-date and trustworthy data. An MDM system is instrumental here, as it carries out the critical work of cleaning, merging, and enriching data to create "golden records." By using master data from the MDM hub, the data fabric's semantic layer can navigate enterprise data assets more effectively, simplifying queries and providing accurate, consistent data for business users.

MDM as a Foundation for Generative AI

Generative AI, based on large language models (LLMs), has the potential to revolutionize various industries. However, LLMs frequently produce unreliable content due to poor-quality training data from publicly available sources. Corporations are now training LLMs on corporate-specific data for better accuracy.

MDM systems provide the high-quality, timely, and accurate data needed to train these LLMs effectively. Companies with well-managed MDM hubs have a competitive advantage, as their AI models perform significantly better with reliable data foundations.

Conclusion

Master data management remains a cornerstone of modern enterprise data architecture. Whether implementing a data fabric or training generative AI, having a well-managed master data hub is essential. The ability to access, clean, and enrich data ensures that enterprises can leverage advanced technologies effectively, driving innovation and maintaining a competitive edge. Investing in MDM is not just a safeguard for data quality; it is a strategic move that underpins numerous modern data initiatives.

Read the full paper on the capabilities and benefits of a modern master data management system here and listen to our podcast.

Learn more about SAP’s solution for master data management, SAP Master Data Governance, at www.sap.com/mdg.

entry.title: How to manage log views and Backoffice permissions in SAP Commerce Cloud
entry.content:
I need assistance with configuring access permissions. Here are my requirements:

  • I would like to give access to logs view only.
  • I would to manage Backoffice permissions.
  • I would like to disable builds and deployments from an user access.

Can I have proper documentation for that? Thanks.


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: How to manage log views and Backoffice permissions in...
entry.content:
I understand you're looking to give access to view only Logs for Production. To do this, you should use the CUSTOMER_LOG_VIEWER. This role allows access to logs including the OpenSearch View for Environments, however, it doesn't provide access to Dynatrace application performance monitoring.
Regarding the builds and deployment request, if the user doesn't have the CUSTOMER_DEVELOPER role in Cloud Portal, it cannot trigger that.
For more detailed information and roles description, you can refer to the Roles in the Cloud Portal documentation.
Additionally, as mentioned in the Backoffice Visibility Controls documentation, an admin user can limit the visibility of Backoffice features and functions to employee users and user groups. You can find more information about Backoffice permissions in the Permissions in The Backoffice Framework documentation.

entry.title: Attachments From Sales Orders are Automatically Added to the Follow-up Customer Invoice
entry.content:
Hello,

Could you please explain why an attachment currently placed on a sales order is automatically added to the invoice and the integration then tries to send the attachment?
The attachment problem is hampering our customer's production operations, as the invoice operator is unable to process invoices with an attachment.


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Attachments From Sales Orders are Automatically Added...
entry.content:
The attachments from Sales Orders are carried to the Customer invoice request and then to the Customer Invoice Document.

This is the expected system behavior.

Business ByDesign currently does not have a functionality to remove the link between attachments on Sales Order and Customer Invoice, therefore it is not possible to change it.

Best Regards,

Marcelo
SAP Product Support

entry.title: Re: US taxing procedure TAXUSJ to 0TXUSX
entry.content:
Dear Narendra,

If you have US tax postings in the production system, the system will not allow a change of tax procedure from TAXUSJ to 0TXUSX. Customers can explore both the internal tax and external tax options in Starter/Quality system before going live, when the tax procedure can still be changed to external. However, once a customer moves into production, changing the tax procedure effectively is not possible because of the existing tax postings.

Please refer to the SAP Note 347260 and also the attached screenshot of the SAP Help.

https://me.sap.com/notes/0003472620

Regards,

Shreya

entry.title: [Reports] How to add missing languages support (e.g. Chinese) in custom reports
entry.content:
Symptom

At Checkout or through the option 'Print & Send', the report screen of a custom JasperSoft report does not show 'Chinese' as a supported language option, so Chinese characters are missing in the report.

Environment

  • SAP Field Service Management
  • JasperSoft Studio 6.2.x

Reproducing the Issue

  1. Log in on the Windows Client (or any other mobile app)

  2. Navigate to the Checkout of the Activity or click on the 3-dots menu and select 'Print & Send'

  3. At the report screen, select the template, format (e.g., PDF), and language

    Actual Behavior:

  4. The language selection is missing 'Chinese', so Chinese characters are not displayed in the report

    Expected Behavior:

  5. Include 'Chinese' in the language selection to enable the display of Chinese characters in the report

Cause

The translation properties files for the 'Chinese' language were missing and needed to be added in JasperSoft Studio.

Resolution

To add 'Chinese' language support to a report in Jaspersoft Studio:

  1. Download the report
  2. Navigate in the Project Explorer to 'Translations.properties' and click on it
  3. At the properties tab with all the languages click on 'New'
  4. Fill out the 'New properties file' with the language 'Chinese (Simplified)', using the language 'zh' and country code 'CN'
  5. Save the translation
  6. Upload the report to the FSM cloud

Once uploaded, on the the report screen at the mobile app the language selection should have the Chinese language as well.  

 

Please note that only the 'SimSun' font is supported, with no planned support for alternatives like 'Noto Sans SC'. Additionally, using multiple fonts, e.g. such as 'Arial' for English and 'Noto Sans SC' for Chinese, is not supported as well. We recommend to open an influence at https://influence.sap.com/sap/ino/#/campaign/1608 if this is desired.

entry.title: Upgrade your SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition following SAP Activate
entry.content:
We just launched the Roadmap Viewer for SAP Activate for RISE with SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition - Upgrade. In this blog post, I want to give you a quick rundown of what you can find there.

Let’s begin with the basics: What is SAP Activate?

SAP Activate is the methodology used to implement SAP solutions. It follows an Agile approach and simplifies project management by breaking it down into manageable tasks across various workstreams, such as Project Management, Solution Adoption, and Customer Team Enablement.

While many tasks are applicable to any project, some are specific to the solution, approach or project type. This blog post will focus on the specific activities to consider at each stage of an SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition upgrade.

This graph gives a quick overview of the task I am talking about:

Let’s take a closer look by phase.

Discover Your Solution

The Discover phase is about what happens before you officially-kick off your SAP S/4HANA project. By the time you finish this phase, your organization should have an SAP S/4HANA upgrade plan, supported by a business case.

Here are some key tasks to focus on during this phase:

Let’s check them out a bit more closely:

Experience the latest SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition

To really understand the potential benefits of the latest SAP S/4HANA version, it helps to have access to an actual SAP S/4HANA system. You can set up a 30-day trial system pretty quickly using the SAP Cloud Appliance Library. It’s hosted on Microsoft Azure, AWS, or Google Cloud, but keep in mind that hosting costs apply, and you’ll need an account with the cloud provider.

Upgrade Readiness Assessment

To plan your project, you must evaluate what it will take to run an SAP S/4HANA upgrade. The SAP Readiness Check for SAP S/4HANA is a super useful, automated tool that helps you assess your current SAP S/4HANA. It identifies simplification items that could affect your system, as well as analyzing custom code, add-ons, business functions, and impacted SAP Fiori apps.

This tool also tells you about compatibility features still in use, which are functionalities created for SAP ERP but available in SAP S/4HANA for a limited time. Just a heads up: usage rights for these features will expire soon, so it’s essential to replace them as part of your upgrade.

 

Prepare your Upgrade

The primary focus during this phase is organizing all the planning details. This is when your project officially begins. This includes defining your objectives and creating a detailed plan. Additionally, you assign members to the project team.

Here are some key tasks to focus on during this phase:

Let’s check some of them more thoroughly.

Transition Planning for Upgrade

  • Evaluate Impacts on SAP Fiori Apps and Other UIs: You should take a look at how SAP Fiori apps and other UIs will be affected in the current SAP S/4HANA system. For that, you’ll analyze which apps are outdated or no longer supported and find out what their replacements are. You'll also document your findings to determine what needs to be done in the upgraded SAP S/4HANA system. This means adding the new apps, getting rid of the old ones, reapplying any extensions, and planning how to help the business users who will be impacted by these changes.
  • Custom Code Analysis: As you are upgrading your SAP S/4HANA, it’s crucial to analyze the existing custom code and document the objects that will be impacted.
  • Choosing the Right Upgrade Approach: Based on your business needs, you should define the best way to upgrade your SAP S/4HANA system. When planning the upgrade, it's important to check out the different approaches and downtime optimization options right from the start since they can affect your timeline, the amount of work involved, and the cost.
  • Planning the upgrade Cycles: To guarantee a seamless upgrade process, it's important to create a well-structured plan. This includes determining the number of cycles you will go through. Ideally, you should aim for at least four cycles: sandbox, development, quality assurance, and final production. If you come across any problems or opt for a particular upgrade method, you may need to add extra cycles.
  • Considering Dual Maintenance: While you’re upgrading your SAP S/4HANA, there might be times when you need to make some changes to the production environment before everything is set in stone. This means you might have to set up a temporary system to handle those changes while the main system is being upgraded. Also, you’ll need to make sure any updates you make sync up with the upgraded systems. So, it’s a good idea to evaluate if you need a dual maintenance process.

Upgrade Preparation

  • Request Additional Temporary Systems:  Request any temporary systems you might need for the SAP S/4HANA upgrade project, keeping in line with the upgrade strategy. This should include things like a sandbox, which gives you a safe space to test changes before you touch the productive landscape. Having a sandbox helps the project team spot potential issues, determine what changes are needed, and plan ahead, which ultimately helps reduce downtime during the actual upgrade. If you ask for these systems early, you'll give the SAP Enterprise Cloud Services team enough time to set them up according to your project timeline.
  • Define Data Validation Tools: When upgrading to SAP S/4HANA, it's super important to make sure your business data remains consistent. You'll want to choose the right tools to keep everything on track and check that the data is in good shape both before and after the upgrade.

Fit to Standard Preparation for specific features

These workshops are a direct result of the upgrade Readiness Assessment, specifically the Conduct Functional Review Workshop during the Discover phase. It includes features that you are replacing, such as items in compatibility scope and new functionalities that you are not currently using but will implement during your upgrade.

The fit-to-standard analysis and design includes the following tasks:

During Prepare, you must focus on the first two:

  • Workshop Preparation: Get ready for the Fit-to-Standard workshops by making sure the relevant processes are set up in the initial system. Each workshop is co-led by an experienced SAP consultant and a business lead. Together, they will develop a workshop schedule detailing the content, agendas, presenters, participants, timings, and locations, ensuring clear boundaries and integration points between workshops.
  • Select the right system: It's super important to choose the right system for these activities. You’ll want to consider options like the SAP S/4HANA Fully-Activated Appliance, or a previously upgraded system (if you have one). Don’t forget to wrap up any pending tasks, like creating sample data, so everything is set for the workshops.

 

Explore the Solution

The Explore phase involves running the first upgrade of a sandbox system. After the technical upgrade, there are a few things to keep in mind, like getting SAP Fiori up and running.

Once the upgrade is done, the project team will do some initial testing to ensure that everything that shouldn’t have been affected remains fine. Additionally, you'll dive deep into the current custom code and review the integrations. This analysis isn’t just about listing what’ll be affected; it’s also about figuring out how and when to switch over to options that fit with clean core principles.

Here are some key tasks to focus on during this phase:

Let’s check some of them more thoroughly.

Upgrade Execution of the Sandbox System

You start by creating an SAP S/4HANA sandbox system from the existing production system and perform a full upgrade. You can't move on to the Realize phase until the sandbox upgrade is successful.

Here’s a quick rundown of the steps involved:

  1. Pre-Upgrade Activities: After cloning the production system, make sure the sandbox meets all technical requirements. Check compatibility with add-ons and tools, and update any necessary SAP Notes. Document everything for future reference, which means creating a Runbook. Before diving into the technical upgrade, tackle any SAP S/4HANA simplification items that need fixing. Also, run and log results from the business data validation reports.
  2. Upgrade: Ask the Enterprise Cloud Services team to run the SUM tool for upgrading your SAP S/4HANA. Yes, SAP will take care of SUM and contact you only if needed😊.
  3. Post-upgrade activities: Follow the “Upgrade Guide for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition” to complete all the technical tasks pending. Also, run the business data validation reports again and compare results with the source system. If there are discrepancies, investigate and ensure it’s ok to proceed.

Additional Activities in the upgraded Sandbox System

After you finish the technical conversion, there are a few steps to make sure everything's ready for the next phase:

  • SAP Fiori activities: After the upgrade, don’t forget to run the activities to ensure that SAP Fiori is working smoothly. Also, make sure to enable the new Fiori apps for testing.
  • Run Initial Testing: Validate that the upgrade didn’t impact any unexpected processes by running your usual end-to-end scenarios. Consider using the new SAP Fiori for some steps to see its value and rethink your user experience strategy.

Fit-to-Standard Workshops for Specific Features

As explained before the fit-to-standard analysis and design process helps to get approval for changes to the project scope and design before moving into the realization phase. These workshops come from the Conversion Readiness Assessment done in the Discover phase.

Just to refresh your mind, the fit-to-standard analysis and design includes the following tasks:

Here are the activities to be conducted during the Explore phase (from 3 to 6):

  • Fit-to-Standard Workshops & Documentation: These workshops help ensure the solution meets the business needs and processes set in the Prepare phase. Business process experts explore functionalities and figure out any additional requirements, which get documented in the project backlog. It's crucial to document all decisions and requirements from the workshops. Technical experts also document all the change requirements in detail.
  • Validating Requirements: Solution architects and project managers work together to ensure all requirements are accurately captured and classified. This helps prioritize which requirements to focus on and decide whether to keep old features or adopt new ones.
  • Design Workshops & documentation: Next, you run a design workshop to document configurations and custom development needed based on the delta requirements prioritized earlier. This is crucial for accurate estimates in the Realize phase.
  • Final Review and Sign-Off: The project architects review the design and make necessary adjustments, ensuring all parties agree on the requirements and proposed solutions. They document and categorize any issues and update the backlog for the Realize phase. Once they have the official approval and necessary documents, the project confidently moves to the next phase.

Custom Code Deep-Dive Analysis

When running an upgrade, an important part of the process is identifying the custom code that will be impacted. Also plan a move following the clean core approach. The main tasks here are:

  • Make a List of Custom Code Objects Affected by the Upgrade: Write down the custom objects you need to tweak if you want them to work after the upgrade. 
  • Make a List of Custom Code Objects That Can Move to Clean Core: Note down which custom objects can be shifted to the new extensibility options.
  • Set Up Coding Guidelines for New Custom Development Requests: Create some development rules so that any new code fits into the clean core approach.

Integration Review and Redesign

At this stage, you should list the integrations you currently have and evaluate which ones may be affected by the system upgrade. After that, create a strategy to address any potential issues or replace those integrations with new technology from SAP Solutions.

  • Analyze Current Integrations: Start by using SAP Readiness Check and the ABAP test cockpit to check all your inbound and outbound integrations. Then, figure out which interfaces are affected.
  • Define the Target Integration: Next, lay out a game plan for replacing outdated technologies with tools like SAP Integration Suite or SAP Process Orchestration. The idea is to move away from old methods like IDocs to more modern options like APIs or OData services to improve functionality and keep maintenance straightforward. While you're focusing on the integrations that will be impacted by the system upgrade, this is also a great chance to create a roadmap for upgrading all your integrations in the long run.
  • Apply Clean Core Principles: When it comes to integration, it's best to stick to standard SAP methods (like OData, SOAP, and events), and make sure you're orchestrating effectively between SAP S/4HANA and any external platforms. Security also needs to be up to date. Prioritize standard integrations and consider custom interfaces only when there’s no other choice.

 

Realize Your Requirements

In the Realize phase, you will upgrade the development and quality systems. You will also implement new requirements based on the design and tweak the impacted custom code as needed. You will set up the necessary integrations. Once everything is adjusted, the project team will jump into the planned testing.

Here are some key tasks to focus on during this phase:

Let’s review them more thoroughly:

Upgrade of the Development System

You must run your upgrade following the Runbook documented in the sandbox system. The steps are the ones you already know:

  1. Pre-Upgrade Activities of the Development System
  2. Upgrade of the Development System
  3. Post-Upgrade Activities of the Development System

Additional Activities in the Upgraded Development System

After completing the technical upgrade of the development system, there are several important tasks you need to address to prepare everything for testing. Here’s a breakdown:

  • SAP Fiori activities: This includes updating the business roles related to replaced apps to reference their successors, creating or reviewing roles to ensure new applications are accessible to end-users, and recreating any necessary user personalization for replaced applications in the new version. Review the extensions of deprecated and obsolete applications in the source version against their successors in the target version, and plan remediation options according to the clean core guidance previously defined.
  • Custom Code Remediation: Next up is fixing the custom code. You’ll start with a prioritized list of the affected objects that may need to be cleaned up or redesigned to fit into a “clean core” concept. This step involves making functional tweaks and optimizing performance, along with potentially refreshing the user experience.
  • Fixing Integrations: You’ll need to make necessary updates to the interfaces and replace any outdated integrations highlighted during the Integration Review phase. This means modifying some custom code, incorporating new standard integrations, and creating workarounds for custom scenarios if standard options aren’t available.
  • Solution Configuration: You’ll configure additional features in iterative cycles, focusing on the business scenarios and process requirements you outlined earlier. This will normally occur in two-week sprints, during which you’ll tackle system setup, documentation, and unit testing according to the agreed-upon backlog. Every change will be documented, and you'll collect all customizing transports along the way.

Upgrade of the Quality System

Before you jump into integration testing, you must upgrade the quality assurance system. Ideally QAS should be a fresh copy of the productive. This is especially important if this will be your last cycle before moving to production.

Going back to the upgrade, you already know how it works, so just make sure to handle all the functional and technical tasks based on the Runbook.

Cutover Preparation

When you're getting ready to upgrade the production system, it's super important to have a solid cutover plan in place. This plan covers all the steps you need to take, from the initial prep work to the final upgrade. Usually, there’s a cutover manager who makes sure everything stays on schedule.

The cutover plan won't dive into all the technical details like a runbook would, but it's a good idea to pull out the key tasks from the runbook to keep everything on track. Don’t forget to include a backup plan just in case you need to roll back the upgrade for any reason.

 

Deploy & Run Your Solution

In this part of the project, you'll do the final upgrade and get the system up and running. Just a heads up, you might have to cut back on some sleep while you're handling Hypercare Support.

Here are some key tasks to focus on during these phases:

Let’s check them out more closely.

Upgrade of the Production System (Cutover)

To wrap up the production system upgrade, you need to tackle all the tasks in the cutover plan that you’ve worked through in the earlier cycles. Just make sure to follow the detailed steps in the runbook.

System Go-Live and Hypercare Support

The system's go-live is the last big step in a successful SAP S/4HANA upgrade. At this point, everything is up and running in the production environment, and then you move into the hypercare phase to help everyone get used to the upgraded system.

Hypercare is the period right after go-live where you're here to tackle any questions or issues that pop up. You’ll be doing daily checks to keep a close eye on how well the new solution is being adopted. It’s super important to see how the system is performing and make any tweaks if needed.

Continuous Improvement and Innovation

You need to set up some IT maintenance processes to continuously improve the solutions. This means regularly rolling out feature packs and support packs to get the latest updates from SAP. It’s important for both the business and IT teams to work together on a planning cycle to decide which new features to implement.

Those improvements include deploying the UX strategy defined during the Prepare Phase and replacing integrations and custom code following the Clean Core approach defined during Explore.

We've come to the end of this blog post.  Just a reminder, this was a quick overview of some of the tasks you can find in the SAP Activate Roadmap Viewer for SAP Activate for RISE with SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition - Upgrade. Check out the details in the Roadmap Viewer to help you plan your upgrade project.

If you want to know more about upgrades, check out these documents:

  • Upgrading SAP S/4HANA—How, Why, and Best Practices
  • Embrace Agility and upgrades for success

I hope you found this blog post helpful. If you did, please give it a "like" or share it. I’d love to hear your thoughts and feedback, so feel free to leave a comment. If you have any questions, just drop them in the Q&A section with the tags SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition. We'll try to get back to you as soon as we can.


Thanks,

Brought to you by the SAP S/4HANA RIG team.

entry.title: Wrong resulting inventory value moved to the Opening balance of the next period.
entry.content:
Resulting inventory value in the first period is not moved to the Opening balance of the second period.


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Wrong resulting inventory value moved to the Opening...
entry.content:
The logic of the Inventory cost history opening balance is that,

Resulting Inventory Quantity - The total inventory quantity that resulted when the business transaction was posted.

Resulting Inventory Value - The total inventory value that resulted when the business transaction was posted.

Capitalized Quantity - The business transaction quantity that was capitalized to inventory. This quantity is used to calculate the Resulting Inventory Quantity.

Capitalized Amount - The business transaction value that was capitalized to inventory. This amount is used to calculate the Resulting Inventory Value.  

This can be explained in the below mentioned scenario with an example.

For the material, with the period: 001/9999

Resulting value: 10.000 USD 
Resulting quantity: 8000 Pcs.

Capitalized value: -100 USD
Capitalized quantity: 0pcs

Calculation: Resulting value : 10,000 USD + Capitalized value -100 USD = 10.100 USD

This is the amount which results in the opening balance of the period: 002/9999. If there is a capitalized value then it can be added with the resulting inventory value and updated in the opening balance of the next period.

entry.title: SAP Developer News, December 5th, 2024
entry.content:
Podcast: https://podcast.opensap.info/sap-developers/2024/12/05/sap-developer-news-december-5th-2024/

DESCRIPTION

Orchestration service of Generative AI Hub

  • Learning Journey on Generative AI Hub: https://learning.sap.com/learning-journeys/solving-your-business-problems-using-prompts-and-llms-in-sap-s-generative-ai-hub/using-generative-ai-hub-sdk-to-leverage-power-of-llms
  • SAP Help Orchestration service of Generative AI Hub on SAP AI Core: https://help.sap.com/docs/sap-ai-core/sap-ai-core-service-guide/orchestration-workflow

New UI for SAP Build Apps

  • Daniel’s blog on new UI: https://community.sap.com/t5/sap-builders-blog-posts/review-new-layout-features-for-sap-build-apps/ba-p/13933240

Updated integration architecture guide and API Management

  • Integration architecture guide: Updated version available now: https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-sap/integration-architecture-guide-updated-version-available-now/ba-p/13930016
  • Please share your experience - new API Management survey: https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-sap/please-share-your-experience-new-api-management-survey/ba-p/13950848

karma-ui5 plugin deprecation and upcoming UI5 & CAP events

  • karma-ui5 deprecation blog post https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-sap/deprecation-of-karma-ui5-plugin/ba-p/13954060
  • UI5ers Live 12 December https://www.linkedin.com/events/ui5erslive-december20247268259526246645760/about/
  • CAP Customer Roundtable 12 December https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-sap/sap-cloud-application-programming-model-eighth-customer-roundtable/ba-p/13951006

CHAPTER TITLES

0:00 Intro

0:10 Orchestration service of Generative AI Hub

1:03 New UI for SAP Build Apps

2:10 Updated integration architecture guide and API Management

3:00 karma-ui5 plugin deprecation and upcoming UI5 & CAP events

Transcription

[Intro] This is the SAP Developer News for December 5th, 2024.

[Nora] There's a lot of talk about the integration of Microsoft Co-pilot and Joule here at UKISUG, but if you want to try out and build your own customized AI use cases, then you should try out Generative AI Hub. We had a session on it on Sunday, where we actually used the orchestration service and the JavaScript SDK and the Python SDK to build our own AI use cases. And what I love most about the orchestration service is that you can use the same code base for all the available models that we have, and you just have to change the model name parameter to try out the different models and compare the results which I think is awesome. We also have a lot of other features in the orchestration service for example the content filtering and the data masking the prompt templating and obviously the grounding feature which you need if you want to build your own RAG use cases and the grounding feature is already available in the API in the next coming week it's also going to be available via the SDKs and I'm super excited to try it out myself let me know what you think once you try it out.

[Daniel] SAP Build Apps updated its design time UI to put front and center the things that you use most often and to hide the things that you use less often or even just once during your project development. So the first thing they did is to update the main menu. So you have just the main parts of building an application, which is the UI, the variables, which I consider part of the logic, and connecting to data resources. Then they moved the preview and build or now publish buttons in the upper right in a place where most IDEs would put them. And finally, they created a much easier way to handle managing pages, creating new pages or navigating to those pages. And that way you don't have to go into the page UI. I've written a blog that gives a review of all the changes, some tips on using them or finding what you need, though, and also my opinion on some of the features and perhaps some of the features I would like to see in the future. So check out the blog, and I'll put the link in the description.

[Antonio] Hola, SAP developers. We're here at the UKISUG Connect event in Birmingham, and I have some quick leads to share with you. First, the Integration Architecture Guide was recently updated. It is structured according to the concepts of the Integration Solution Advisor Methodology. And it provides you guidelines about all things integration, including process integration, event-driven integration, API integrations, data integration, and user integration. So make sure to check it out. Also, we want your feedback regarding the API management capability. There is a new server available and we want you to learn more about how you're implementing and benefiting from the capability, or even why you're not using API management. So, we're looking forward to hearing back from you.

[DJ] I've just got time for some news items on count. And UI5, first UI5. As some of you already may be aware, the Karma UI5 plugin is deprecated. This follows the deprecation of the Karma test runner itself upon which the plugin is based. But don't despair, there are plenty of other testing options available, including the UI5 test runner. And also, there's an event next week on Thursday the 12th of December, UI5ers Live, where this deprecation is discussed along with alternative options. And talking of next week, Thursday the 12th of December, there's a second event that day for CAP developers. It's another CAP customer round table with lots of news and updates from the CAP team and discussion of topics from the community. That's you. So check out Oliver's blog post on the deprecation and also the event pages. Links, as always, are in the description.

entry.title: Re: Real User Monitoring in CALM operations. Monitor the...
entry.content:
Hi, wimsy, In the Operations use cases, such as Real User Monitoring, there is a Scope selection option to enable any service/system you want to monitor.  Each user can set the systems/services they want to monitor within each use case.  (Know that activating these components does affect your Fair Usage data allowance.-> see KBA 3499485 - How much does SAP Cloud ALM cost?)

Go to Real User Monitoring and click Scope.  Search for the S/4HANA system/service you want to monitor, check the box in front of the entry and click Apply. 

Click Configuration and ensure Data Collection is ON.

I hope this is helpful to you.

Best regards,
Paula

entry.title: Intercompany Purchase Order NBIC does not allow Item Category L nor Free of Charge orders
entry.content:
Hello, 

I am experiencing a number of issues when creating a purchase order of type NBIC.

Case 1

The customer’s need would be to carry out a subcontracting stock transfer order between plants. 

Let me explain. To date, plant A creates a purchase order for a certain part number towards plant B (belonging to another company code). At the same time, plant A sends components to plant B for the production of the part number in the line item of the purchase order. It is therefore a true subcontracting but of intercompany type.   

To replicate this case, I tried to create a purchase order of type NBIC and in the line item I entered a part number for which there is a BOM. To make the part number components linked to the purchase order, I specified in the field Item Category the value L = Subcontracting. The system retrieved me the following error message: 

Item Category L not supported for Purchase Order document type NBIC in SIT

Message no. MEPO178

Diagnosis

The combination of document type &V1& and item category &V3& is not supported in the context of the chosen Stock Transfer Process. The used item category &V3& is not fitting to the assigned Stock Transfer Process.

If you want to use Stock Transfer Process with valuated stock in transit the purchase order document you are trying to use is of type UB or UB2.In order to support the Stock Transfer Process with valuated stock in transit a different purchase order document type (e.g. NBCR, NBC2, NBC3) must be used instead.
If you want to use an Advanced Intercompany Stock Transfer Process by using purchase order document type NBIC, you should only use the supported item categories for this process. Please adjust the item category.

Procedure

Check your input and change the document type and item category if necessary.

Entering the Implementation Activities app, I went to check the allowed Item Categories for the NBIC document type, and the item category L is present. 

 

Then, why do I get this error message?

Case 2

To find an alternative to Case 1, which would remain the ideal flow, I thought of managing the sending of components as an additional intercompany flow, inverse to the previous case. 

Meaning, plant B creates a purchase order at price 0 (by marking the flag "Free of Charge" for each line item) towards plant A (which in this case would represent the supplier). All to manage the sending of components, which should be sent free of charge. 

In this case, the system retrieved the following error message:

With GR-based invoice verification please enter GR and IR indicators

Message no. 06160

Diagnosis

The indicator for goods-receipt-(GR)-based invoice verification, has been set but either the goods receipt indicator or the invoice receipt indicator has not been set.

For GR-based invoice verification, a goods receipt (GR) and an invoice receipt (IR) are necessary.

Procedure

Make sure your entries are correct.

It seems that the problem is in the presence of the GR-based Invoice flag but I cannot remove it in the Purchase Order.

I thought that the problem was in the configuration of the Business partner of "Plant B". Entering the Supplier role, within the Purchasing view, specifying the Purchasing Organization of the plant A, I tried to remove either the GR-based Invoice flag and to mark Grant Free Goods flag, without successful outcome. 

Can you please support us in solving this issue?

Thank you,

Nicola

entry.title: Re: OData service for retrieving units of measure in SAP...
entry.content:
Some time ago I found that currency and unit-of-measure information is offered by all OData V4 services, which are provided as a "service group", which includes a "common" node for the above and the main node with actual information.

Let's consider this V4 service for retrieval of measurement documents.


To retrieve units-of-measure (optionally in a language of choice):
/sap/opu/odata4/sap/API_MEASUREMENTDOCUMENT/default/iwbep/common/0001/UnitsOfMeasure?sap-language=DE

To retrieve actual measurement documents:
/sap/opu/odata4/sap/API_MEASUREMENTDOCUMENT/srvd_a2x/sap/measurementdocument/0001/MeasurementDocument
